methods.description: Protocol for performing Amplified Fragment Length Polymorphism (AFLP) analysis on DNA samples, including restriction-ligation, pre-selective and selective PCR, fragment separation, and scoring. This method was applied to a subset of 54 individuals plus 22 duplicate samples.

steps.description: Approximately 300 ng of DNA was digested and ligated in a 15 μl reaction mix containing 1 × T4-ligase buffer and 1 × ATP solution (Bioline, London, UK), 50 mM NaCl, 0.75 μg BSA, 1.5 U T4-ligase (Bioline), 1 U MseI and 5 U EcoRI (New England Biolabs), and 0.37μM of EcoRI-adapter and 3.67 μM of MseI-adapter.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform DNA restriction and adapter ligation for AFLP using EcoRI and MseI enzymes and specific adapters.

steps.description: The reaction mix was incubated at 37˚C for 3 h.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate the restriction-ligation reaction.

steps.description: followed by an inactivation step at 65˚C for 10 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Inactivate enzymes after restriction-ligation.

steps.description: The restriction-ligation product was subsequently diluted ten-fold.
steps.searchable_summary: Dilute the restriction-ligation product.

steps.description: For the pre-selective PCR reaction, 2.5 μl of the diluted restriction-ligation product were used in a total reaction volume of 12.5 μl which contained 10 × PCR buffer II (Applied Biosystems), 2 mM MgCl 2 , 0.8 mM dNTP mix, 0.2 μM EcoRI-A primer (5'-GACTGCGTACCAATTCA-A-3'), 0.2 μM MseI-C primer (5'-GATGAGTCCTGAGTAAC-C-3') and 0.25 U AmpliTaq polymerase (Applied Biosystems).
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare and perform pre-selective PCR amplification.

steps.description: The reactions were held at 72˚C for 2 min, followed by 20 cycles of 94˚C for 20 s, 56˚C for 30 s, and 72˚C for 2 min, with a final 30 s extension at 60˚C
steps.searchable_summary: Execute pre-selective PCR thermal cycling program.

steps.description: and were subsequently diluted ten-fold.
steps.searchable_summary: Dilute the pre-selective PCR product.

steps.description: For selective PCR, 2.5 μl of the diluted pre-selective PCR product were used as a template in a total reaction volume of 12.5 μl. The PCR master mix contained 1 × GoldTaq buffer (Applied Biosystems), 2.5 mM MgCl 2 , 0.8mM dNTP mix, 0.2μM Mse primer, 0.08μM EcoRI fluorescence-labeled primer (EcoRI-ACG (NED)/MseI-CTC, EcoRI-AAG(6-FAM)/MseI-CTA, EcoRI-AGC(VIC)/MseI-CTG,EcoRI-AGG(NED)/MseI-CAT; EcoRI-AAC(6-FAM)/MseI-CAG and EcoRI-ACC(VIC)/MseI-CTC), and 0.5 U Ampli-Taq Gold (Applied Biosystems).
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare and perform selective PCR amplification with fluorescent primers.

steps.description: The reactions were held at 95˚C for 5 min, followed by 13 cycles at 94˚C for 30 s, a touch down cycle of 65˚C to 56˚C (-0.7˚C per cycle) for 1 min and 72˚C for 1 min, followed by another 23 cycles at 94˚C for 30 s, 56˚C for 1 min and 72˚C for 1.5 min, with a final 8 min extension at 72˚C.
steps.searchable_summary: Execute selective PCR thermal cycling program with touchdown.

steps.description: Differentially fluorescence-labeled PCR products and GS600 LIZ size standards (Applied Biosystems) were multiplexed
steps.searchable_summary: Combine labeled PCR products with size standards for analysis.

steps.description: and fragments were separated on a 3730 DNA Analyzer (Applied Biosystems). In each run, a total of 96 samples were analyzed, including one negative control and several other repeats (altogether 37%), as recommended by Bonin et al. [36].
steps.searchable_summary: Separate DNA fragments by size using capillary electrophoresis on a DNA Analyzer.

steps.description: Raw data were visualized and the fragments manually scored using GeneMarker v1.97 (Soft Genetics, State College, USA).
steps.searchable_summary: Visualize raw fragment data and manually score AFLP fragments (presence/absence) using GeneMarker software.

steps.description: Processed data were exported as a presence/absence matrix.
steps.searchable_summary: Export scored AFLP data into a binary presence/absence matrix format.

methods.description: Statistical and computational methods applied to analyze cpDNA sequence data and AFLP presence/absence data, including data processing, diversity calculations, population structure analysis, and correlation tests.

steps.description: Indels in the cpDNA sequences were manually coded for presence and absence using the approach described by Simmons and Ochoterena [37]
steps.searchable_summary: Manually code cpDNA sequence indels into binary presence/absence format.

steps.description: and treated as single polymorphic sites.
steps.searchable_summary: Represent coded cpDNA indels as single polymorphic sites for downstream analysis.
outputs: Processed cpDNA data including indels as polymorphic sites

steps.description: After manual coding of the indels and removal of the repeated sequence motifs, the total length of the alignment was reduced to 244 bp, and 10 parsimony-informative sites were considered.
steps.searchable_summary: Remove repeated sequence motifs from cpDNA alignment.

steps.description: Seven haplotypes were identified
steps.searchable_summary: Identify distinct haplotypes from processed cpDNA data.

steps.description: A statistical parsimony network among cpDNA haplotypes was reconstructed using TCS v1.2 [38] with a default connection limit of 95%.
steps.searchable_summary: Build a statistical parsimony network for cpDNA haplotypes using TCS software.

steps.description: Haplotypes were then plotted as pie charts on the map of West Africa using the compiled site co-ordinates to show the distribution of haplotypes.
steps.searchable_summary: Visualize cpDNA haplotype distribution on a map using pie charts at sampling site coordinates.

steps.description: Haplotype diversity (h) [39] and nucleotide diversity (π) [40] of populations were calculated using MEGA v5 [41] and DnaSP v5.10.1 [42].
steps.searchable_summary: Compute genetic diversity indices (haplotype diversity 'h' and nucleotide diversity 'π') for cpDNA data per population using MEGA and DnaSP software.

steps.description: After removing fragments with an error rate of more than 15%, 374 clearly scorable fragments sized from 100 to 591bp were considered for further analyses
steps.searchable_summary: Filter AFLP fragments from the presence/absence matrix based on an error rate threshold (>15%).

steps.description: For the AFLP dataset several statistical parameters such as total number of fragments, proportion of polymorphic fragments, number of private fragments, and Nei's gene diversity for the whole sampling as well as for particular populations [40] were computed using the R-script AFLPdat [43].
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate descriptive statistics and genetic diversity parameters (total fragments, polymorphism, private fragments, Nei's diversity) for AFLP data using the AFLPdat R-script.
outputs: AFLP statistical parameters (fragment counts, polymorphism, private fragments, Nei's diversity)

steps.description: Main trends in genetic variation among individual genotypes were visualized by principal coordinate analysis based on Jaccard distances (PCoA) calculated using PASTv2.7 [44].
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate pairwise Jaccard distances for the AFLP presence/absence matrix.

steps.description: Main trends in genetic variation among individual genotypes were visualized by principal coordinate analysis based on Jaccard distances (PCoA) calculated using PASTv2.7 [44].
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Principal Coordinate Analysis (PCoA) on the AFLP Jaccard distance matrix and visualize the results using PAST software.

steps.description: For both, cpDNA and AFLP datasets, F-statistics, AMOVA and Mantel tests (based on pairwise population F ST matrix) were calculated in Arlequin v3.1 [45]
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate F-statistics (e.g., FST) for both cpDNA and AFLP datasets using Arlequin software.

steps.description: For both, cpDNA and AFLP datasets, F-statistics, AMOVA and Mantel tests (based on pairwise population F ST matrix) were calculated in Arlequin v3.1 [45]
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Analysis of Molecular Variance (AMOVA) for both cpDNA and AFLP datasets using Arlequin software.
outputs: AMOVA results (variance components, p-values) for cpDNA
AMOVA results (variance components, p-values) for AFLP

steps.description: For both, cpDNA and AFLP datasets, F-statistics, AMOVA and Mantel tests (based on pairwise population F ST matrix) were calculated in Arlequin v3.1 [45]
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Mantel tests to correlate pairwise population FST matrices with geographic distance matrix for both cpDNA and AFLP datasets using Arlequin software.
outputs: Mantel test results (correlation coefficient rM, p-value) for cpDNA
Mantel test results (correlation coefficient rM, p-value) for AFLP

steps.description: and the significance value tested using a nonparametric permutation test following the method of Excoffier et al. [46].
steps.searchable_summary: Test the statistical significance of F-statistics, AMOVA, and Mantel test results using a nonparametric permutation test in Arlequin.
outputs: Permutation test p-values for Mantel tests
